1. Remote Access & Server Setup (2 hours)

Installed and configured Visual Studio Code, Positron IDE, and Git on the remote server to ensure a consistent development workflow.

Performed full system health check: CPU, GPU, memory, storage, network/IP connectivity.
Verified all instruments and peripherals are functioning correctly.
Installed the GPU drivers.

Reviewed and configured sudo permissions for secure access.

4. Data Transfer & Reference Genome Preparation (2 hours)

Explored secure data upload methods from SharePoint to the server without exposing IP addresses or downloading to local machine.
Two viable options identified: manual download via X11 forwarding, or using Dropbox synchronization.

Downloaded and prepared GENCODE v45 (GRCh38) as the local reference genome (fasta + gtf files), including MD5 checksums for reproducibility.

5. Data Synchronization (1 hour)

Installed Dropbox on the remote server and synchronized all sequencing data for secure backup and access.

6. Project Communication & Planning (1 hour)

Discussed project priorities and timelines with the team.
Clarified which tasks/services require IP exposure.
Explained alternative file-sharing options that do not require IP access (e.g., Dropbox).

7. Nextflow RNA-seq Pipeline Preparation (3 hours)

Built a standardized, reusable Nextflow RNA-seq workflow.
Documented clear the steps and instructions so other team members can run the pipeline by simply following the provided script.

8. Quality Control & Preliminary Analysis (4 hours)

Performed pre-alignment quality control (QC) on raw data (e.g., adapter content, base quality, rRNA contamination).
Compared results against previous custom QC reports.
Analyzed potential causes of observed differences and discussed next steps for downstream analysis.

9. Standard RNA-seq Workflow Execution (4 hours)

Ran the full Nextflow RNA-seq pipeline (total runtime ~8 hours; hands-on time ~3 hours).
Investigated and documented the primary data source (species, DNA/RNA type).

Period: March 27, 2026 – April 2, 2026
Prepared by: Gao Zhen
Total Hours Worked: 26 hours
1. Tropaeolum majus Genome Reference Preparation (4 hours)

Downloaded the GENCODE reference genome and annotation files for Tropaeolum majus.
Converted the annotation files to standard GTF format.
Modified the naive annotation file to ensure full compatibility with the GENCODE format.
Built genome indexes using three tools: SAMtools, STAR, and BWA-MEM2.

2. Separation of Contaminated RNA-seq Samples (6 hours)

Identified that five plant RNA-seq samples were contaminated with sgRNA library samples due to identical i7/i5 indexes.
Conducted in-depth analysis of contamination patterns, read distribution, and possible separation strategies.
Designed, tested, and implemented an effective separation method based on insert-size differences.
Successfully separated the contaminated samples and merged the clean plant samples with other normal samples for downstream analysis.

3. Project Communication & Planning (3 hours)

Held ongoing discussions with the team regarding project challenges and customer requirements, and adjusted priorities accordingly.
Clearly explained experimental capabilities, root causes of observed issues, proposed solutions, and provided realistic timelines.

4. Investigation of Scattered Sample Distribution in the UIC Project (7 hours)

Performed comprehensive quality checks across all processing dimensions and steps for each sample.
Identified the most significant contributing factors to the scattered distribution.
Conducted targeted analyses to validate the initial hypothesis, supported by statistical results and visualizations.

5. Nextflow Rerun to Rescue the UIC Project (6 hours)

Reconfigured and re-executed the Nextflow pipeline to remove low-quality reads.
Redid subsequent analyses to generate updated PCA plots and differential expression (DE) results.

Weekly Work Report
Period: April 10, 2026 – April 16, 2026
Prepared by: Gao Zhen
Total Hours Worked: 16 hours

1. Downstream Analysis for LZJ Project Nextflow Output (3 hours)

Performed standard PCA and differential expression (DE) analyses for the LZJ project.
Added volcano plots and heatmap visualizations to the RNA-seq pipeline.

2. Enhancement of Pipeline Plotting and Reporting Capabilities (3 hours)

Improved R plotting functionalities within the Positron IDE.
Configured Quarto (.qmd) to enable direct generation of customized reports in HTML, PDF, and Word formats.

3. scRNA-seq Pipeline Preparation (4 hours)

Reviewed and executed the downstream standard single-cell RNA-seq pipeline in both R and Python environments.
Verified output data formats and plotting capabilities in each environment.
Compared results, figures, advantages, and limitations between the R and Python implementations.

4. Nextflow Shotgun Metagenomics Pipeline Preparation (6 hours)

Researched and prepared pipelines for shotgun metagenomics data analysis.
Compared the two primary analytical strategies: read-based profiling versus assembly-based analysis.
Evaluated mainstream tools and workflows for the following key steps:
1) QC & Preprocessing and host gene removal: fastp, Bowtie2, and SAMtools.
2) Taxonomic profiling: MetaPhlAn versus Kraken2.
3) Functional profiling: HUMAnN for gene families, pathway abundance, and pathway coverage.
4) Assembly: MEGAHIT versus SPAdes.
5) Binning and reconstruction of individual genomes: MetaBAT2, MaxBin2, and CONCOCT.
6) Unification of genomes from multiple binning tools: metaWRAP versus DAS Tool.
7) Quality assessment for completeness and contamination: CheckM2.


Total Hours Worked: Approximately 16 hours
